description:
<title>Incident-Response Data Reduction: CSV & JSON Correlation</title>

    <scenario>
        You are an on-call incident responder who has just received a small bundle of
        triage artefacts from a potentially compromised host.  
        All material has been copied to the directory  
        /home/user/ir_data.  
        Your job is to extract and interrogate the data, then create a concise,
        machine-readable report for fellow responders.
    </scenario>

    <initial_state>
        1. /home/user/ir_data/logs_bundle.zip – a ZIP archive that contains three files  
           • auth.log.csv – authentication audit events  
           • network.log.csv – outbound network connections  
           • processes.json – a point-in-time process listing  
        2. /home/user/ir_data/blacklist_ips.txt – one IPv4 address per line, listing
           command-and-control endpoints that the SOC currently blocks.  
        3. /home/user/ir_data/suspicious_proc.txt – plain-text list of **exact** process
           names (one per line) that warrant extra scrutiny.
    </initial_state>

    <goal>
        Build the following four artefacts under /home/user/ir_data/summary
        (create the directory if it does not exist).  File names **and column order /
        JSON structure must match exactly**; automated grading is case-sensitive.

        1. failed_login_counts.csv  
           • Format: header line followed by zero-or-more data lines, **comma-separated**  
           • Header: username,failed_attempts  
           • Each subsequent row contains one username plus the integer count of
             failed login attempts for that user, pulled from auth.log.csv.  
           • Only include users whose status column equals “failed”.  
           • Sort rows in descending order of failed_attempts; break ties with
             ascending username (ASCIIbetical).  

        2. blacklisted_connections.csv  
           • Format: header line followed by zero-or-more data lines, **comma-separated**  
           • Header (exactly this order): timestamp,src_ip,dst_ip,dst_port,protocol  
           • Include every row from network.log.csv whose dst_ip appears in
             blacklist_ips.txt. Preserve the **original chronological order** found in
             network.log.csv.

        3. suspicious_processes_found.json  
           • A valid JSON array. Each element is the full JSON object from
             processes.json whose process_name field exactly matches (case-sensitive)
             one of the entries in suspicious_proc.txt.  
           • Preserve the original ordering of items in processes.json.  
           • The file must end with a single newline.

        4. incident_report.json  
           • A single JSON object with the fixed keys shown below, written on one
             line (no pretty-printing / no spaces). The agent should compute the
             integer values dynamically:  
               {
                 "failed_login_totals": <total number of failed login rows>,
                 "users_with_failed_logins": <unique usernames with at least one failure>,
                 "blacklisted_connection_count": <rows written to blacklisted_connections.csv>,
                 "suspicious_process_count": <objects in suspicious_processes_found.json>
               }
           • Exactly this key order. File must end with a newline.

        After creating the four files, verify their presence and contents, then exit
        cleanly.  No additional files should be created in /home/user/ir_data/summary.
    </goal>

    <hints>
        • Expect to use between five and ten shell commands (e.g., unzip, awk, sort,
          uniq, jq, etc.).  
        • Ensure /home/user/ir_data/summary exists before writing output.  
        • Remember to keep headers in the generated CSVs.  
        • Pay special attention to sorting requirements for failed_login_counts.csv.  
        • Use tools that maintain input order when required (e.g., grep without sort).
    </hints>
truth:
<!--
        The autograder will provision the following files **before** the agent starts.

        Directory: /home/user/ir_data

        1. logs_bundle.zip (SHA256: 651c3c0d504a08d97973e1f5ce8c4c9fc9b4c4d12dc23496fd3d8e63689eaf71)
           ├── auth.log.csv
           │   timestamp,username,status,source_ip
           │   2024-06-01T10:00:01Z,alice,success,10.0.0.2
           │   2024-06-01T10:05:22Z,bob,failed,10.0.0.3
           │   2024-06-01T10:06:30Z,bob,failed,10.0.0.3
           │   2024-06-01T10:07:55Z,charlie,failed,192.168.1.5
           │   2024-06-01T10:09:11Z,dave,success,172.16.0.4
           │   2024-06-01T10:10:42Z,bob,failed,10.0.0.3
           │   2024-06-01T10:11:53Z,alice,failed,10.0.0.2
           │   2024-06-01T10:12:14Z,charlie,failed,192.168.1.5
           │   2024-06-01T10:13:27Z,erin,failed,10.0.0.6
           │
           ├── network.log.csv
           │   timestamp,src_ip,dst_ip,dst_port,protocol
           │   2024-06-01T10:05:01Z,10.0.0.3,8.8.8.8,53,udp
           │   2024-06-01T10:05:05Z,10.0.0.3,203.0.113.99,443,tcp
           │   2024-06-01T10:07:44Z,192.168.1.5,104.26.10.78,80,tcp
           │   2024-06-01T10:08:33Z,10.0.0.2,198.51.100.42,22,tcp
           │   2024-06-01T10:09:50Z,10.0.0.6,203.0.113.99,80,tcp
           │   2024-06-01T10:11:02Z,10.0.0.2,192.0.2.15,443,tcp
           │
           └── processes.json
               [
                 {"pid":123,"process_name":"sshd","user":"root"},
                 {"pid":234,"process_name":"apache2","user":"www-data"},
                 {"pid":345,"process_name":"nc","user":"bob"},
                 {"pid":456,"process_name":"python3","user":"charlie"},
                 {"pid":567,"process_name":"malware_x","user":"erin"}
               ]

        2. blacklist_ips.txt
           203.0.113.99
           198.51.100.42

        3. suspicious_proc.txt
           nc
           malware_x

        The expected correct output files are:

        /home/user/ir_data/summary/failed_login_counts.csv
            username,failed_attempts
            bob,3
            charlie,2
            alice,1
            erin,1

        /home/user/ir_data/summary/blacklisted_connections.csv
            timestamp,src_ip,dst_ip,dst_port,protocol
            2024-06-01T10:05:05Z,10.0.0.3,203.0.113.99,443,tcp
            2024-06-01T10:08:33Z,10.0.0.2,198.51.100.42,22,tcp
            2024-06-01T10:09:50Z,10.0.0.6,203.0.113.99,80,tcp

        /home/user/ir_data/summary/suspicious_processes_found.json
            [{"pid":345,"process_name":"nc","user":"bob"},{"pid":567,"process_name":"malware_x","user":"erin"}]\n

        /home/user/ir_data/summary/incident_report.json
            {"failed_login_totals":7,"users_with_failed_logins":4,"blacklisted_connection_count":3,"suspicious_process_count":2}\n
    -->
